title: DHS OIG Forced to Pause 85% of ICE Audits Due to Funding Lapse
excerpt: The DHS Office of Inspector General suspended approximately 85% of its active audits and inspections, including probes into ICE excessive force and detention conditions, after a partial government shutdown cut off its appropriations. Seven investigations directly related to ICE conduct were halted, including the watchdog's review of whether ICE holds personnel accountable for excessive use of force.
content:
A partial government shutdown in February 2026 deprived the DHS Office of Inspector General (OIG) of the funding needed to continue most of its active oversight operations, halting critical reviews at a moment of intense public scrutiny of ICE conduct.

  • Paused investigations included the OIG's audit to determine "whether ICE investigates allegations of use of excessive force and holds personnel accountable."
  • Unannounced inspections of ICE detention facilities to assess compliance with detention standards and conditions were also suspended.
  • A CBP domestic operations review and other ICE-related probes were similarly halted.
  • The OIG confirmed that criminal investigators were exempted from furlough and continued working, but routine audits and inspections stopped.

The OIG had launched eight oversight investigations into ICE and CBP in January 2026 following the Minneapolis shootings. The funding lapse meant those reviews were interrupted even as ICE operations continued at high tempo.
source: Fox News Digital / DHS OIG Spokesperson
